name: Ignatius of Antioch
title: Bishop of Antioch & Martyr
bio: Third bishop of Antioch and early church father. Condemned to die in Rome, he was fed to wild beasts in the Colosseum. Wrote seven profound letters to churches while en route to his execution.
faith: Ignatius eagerly embraced martyrdom, writing: 'I am God's wheat, ground fine by the lion's teeth to be made purest bread for Christ.' His letters emphasize church unity, the Eucharist, and the reality of Christ's incarnation.

sources.title: The Epistle of Ignatius to the Romans
sources.note: Ignatius's most personal letter, written en route to martyrdom, expressing his longing to be 'ground by the teeth of wild beasts' for Christ

sources.title: The Seven Epistles of Ignatius
sources.note: All seven authentic letters of Ignatius, foundational documents of early Christian theology on church unity and the Eucharist

sources.title: Early Church Texts: Ignatius of Antioch
sources.note: Translations and commentary on Ignatius's epistles with historical context

tagline: I am God's wheat, ground fine by the lion's teeth
